item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the feedback, I will fix the mistake. Can you give me tips to improve my dialogues?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interviews took me out of it pretty badly. No vote for now. You also refer to 5817-B as -C in the first interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the second iteration of the page, 5817 is sometimes called SCP-????

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I tried to improve the first part's dialogues on my sandbox (http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/psychlantern). Are they better?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That message at the end nearly got me in making me think that this was going to be turned into an explainer SCP.
Then I read the small text. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. I believe that focusing on the President's awareness of his own anomalous provenance has a great deal of potential in terms of narrative and character development. Given that this is already a dialogue-heavy article, I could see this effectively explore themes such as self-doubt, legitimacy, and national sovereignty in the context of the colonial heritage of the nations of the Caribbean/Americas. Of course, this is speaking in broad terms; how this may look like in terms of the specific progression of the story is up to you, but I feel like the pieces are here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the feedback! I really want this idea to succeed so any help is appreciated.
I have received complaints about the interviews and I'm currently working on them at my sandbox page. As for the President questioning its memories, do you think I should expand on that part to make the piece more interesting?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm pretty sure the semicolon use is wrong in this instance. Author, I would suggest just dropping "however", such that the sentence reads "No attempts to amnesticize it were successful", or perhaps even better, "All attempts at amnesticization of the entity were unsuccessful", since the use of "it" feels too informal.

As for the piece itself, I unfortunately downvoted. It's not a bad premise, but the execution is lacking. The narrative doesn't go anywhere interesting, opting for a straightforward escalation instead, and the one potentially interesting thread of the President doubting his own memories/existence is ultimately abandoned in favour of the aforementioned narrative. Moreover, the interviews fall flat for me, since none of the characters have a particularly distinctive voice. They all sound too similar to one another.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for reporting grammar errors, I already fixed them now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A nice read, I really enjoyed the offset. That was well-done. I gave you a +1.

Btw, in your first interview,

Closing Statement: After the interview, subject was taken to a humanoid containment cell in Site-68. No attempts to amnesticize it, however, were sucessful.

the comma before the however should be a semicolon and the sucessful is spelled with two c's.
I do not understand the final part why the anomaly is explained?

item.title: Long live the republic of kingston
item.content:encoded: All around fun read. Glory to the republic and our President!!

item.title: SCP-5817: There is no place like the Republic of Kingston
item.content:encoded:
My first published page! I hope you like it.

Special thanks to RockTeethMothEyes and TheMightyMcB for greenlighting my idea and also to rattles, SharpEmbrace and everyone else that helped me with improving the original draft.

Image sources:

  • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Flag_of_Afghanistan_(alternative_version).svg (edited into the Kingston Flag)
  • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:LocationAmericas.png (edited into the 2 maps)

And here's a fun fact:
the geographical coordinates of Kingston, Capital of Jamaica, are 17°58′17″N 76°47′35″W
